name: CWE-89: SQL Injection
description: The software constructs all or part of an SQL command using externally-influenced input from an upstream component, but it does not neutralize or incorrectly neutralizes special elements that could modify the intended SQL command when it is sent to a downstream component.
fix: Use parameterized queries (prepared statements) instead of string concatenation. Example: db.query('SELECT * FROM users WHERE id = ?', [userId])

name: CWE-79: Cross-Site Scripting (XSS)
description: The software does not neutralize or incorrectly neutralizes user-controllable input before it is placed in output that is used as a web page that is served to other users.
fix: Encode all user input before rendering in HTML. Use Content-Security-Policy header. Use frameworks that auto-escape (React, Angular). Example: escapeHtml(userInput)

name: CWE-22: Path Traversal
description: The software uses external input to construct a pathname that is intended to identify a file or directory that is located underneath a restricted parent directory, but the software does not properly neutralize special elements within the pathname that can cause the pathname to resolve to a location that is outside of the restricted directory.
fix: Validate and sanitize file paths. Use allowlist of allowed files. Use path.resolve() and check if result starts with allowed directory. Never trust user input for file paths.

name: CWE-78: OS Command Injection
description: The software constructs all or part of an OS command using externally-influenced input from an upstream component, but it does not neutralize or incorrectly neutralizes special elements that could modify the intended OS command when it is sent to a downstream component.
fix: Avoid shell commands. Use built-in language functions. If needed, use execFile with array arguments. Never use user input directly in shell commands.

name: CWE-918: Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF)
description: The web server receives a URL or similar request from an upstream component and retrieves the contents of this URL, but it does not sufficiently ensure that the request is being sent to the expected destination.
fix: Implement allowlist of allowed domains/IPs that can be accessed. Block private IP ranges (10.x, 172.16-31.x, 192.168.x, 169.254.169.254). Validate and sanitize all URLs before fetching.

name: CWE-611: XML External Entity (XXE)
description: The software processes an XML document that can contain XML entities with URIs that resolve to documents outside of the intended sphere of control, causing the software to embed incorrect documents into its output.
fix: Disable external entity processing in XML parsers. Use XXE-safe parser configurations. Example: DocumentBuilderFactory.setFeature('http://apache.org/xml/features/disallow-doctype-decl', true)

name: CWE-502: Deserialization of Untrusted Data
description: The application deserializes untrusted data without sufficiently verifying that the resulting data will be valid, allowing attackers to modify the state or flow of the execution.
fix: Avoid deserializing untrusted data. Use safe serialization formats (JSON). Implement integrity checks. Use allowlist of allowed classes.

name: CWE-787: Out-of-bounds Write
description: The software writes data past the end, or before the beginning, of the intended buffer.
fix: Use bounds checking. Use memory-safe languages. Use safe string functions (strncpy vs strcpy). Enable compiler protections (ASLR, DEP, stack canaries).

name: CWE-287: Improper Authentication
description: When an actor claims to have a given identity, the software does not prove or insufficiently proves that the claim is correct.
fix: Implement multi-factor authentication. Use strong password policies. Implement account lockout. Use secure session management. Never expose authentication status in responses.

name: CWE-434: Unrestricted Upload of File with Dangerous Type
description: The software allows the attacker to upload or transfer files of dangerous types that can be automatically processed within the product's environment.
fix: Validate file type by content, not extension. Store uploads outside webroot. Scan with antivirus. Use allowlist of allowed MIME types. Rename files on upload.

name: CWE-200: Exposure of Sensitive Information to an Unauthorized Actor
description: The product exposes sensitive information to an actor that is not explicitly authorized to have access to that information.
fix: Remove debug information from production. Use generic error messages. Don't expose stack traces. Implement proper access controls on sensitive endpoints.

name: CWE-522: Insufficiently Protected Credentials
description: The product transmits or stores authentication credentials using an insecure method that is susceptible to unauthorized interception and/or retrieval.
fix: Use bcrypt/argon2 for password hashing. Never log credentials. Use HTTPS everywhere. Store secrets in vaults, not code. Implement proper key rotation.

name: CWE-306: Missing Authentication for Critical Function
description: The software does not perform any authentication for functionality that requires a provable user identity or consumes a significant amount of resources.
fix: Add authentication to all sensitive endpoints. Implement proper authorization checks. Use middleware to protect routes. Never trust client-side authentication.

name: CWE-862: Missing Authorization
description: The software does not perform an authorization check when an actor attempts to access a resource or perform an action.
fix: Implement RBAC (Role-Based Access Control). Check permissions on every request. Use attribute-based access control (ABAC). Implement deny-by-default.

name: CWE-352: Cross-Site Request Forgery (CSRF)
description: The web application does not, or can not, sufficiently verify whether a well-formed, valid, consistent request was intentionally provided by the user who submitted the request.
fix: Use CSRF tokens on all state-changing forms. Set SameSite=Strict/Lax on cookies. Implement double-submit cookie pattern. Use custom headers for API calls.

name: CWE-601: URL Redirection to Untrusted Site (Open Redirect)
description: A web application accepts a user-controlled input that specifies a link to an external site, and uses that link in a Redirect. This simplifies phishing attacks.
fix: Validate redirect URLs against allowlist. Use relative URLs only. Warn users before external redirects. Implement redirect confirmation page.

name: CWE-943: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements in Data Query Logic
description: The software does not neutralize or incorrectly neutralizes special elements that could modify data query logic when it is sent to a downstream component.
fix: Use parameterized queries. Implement query allowlists. Validate all query parameters. Use ORM with built-in protection.

name: CWE-1321: Improperly Controlled Modification of Object Prototype Attributes (Prototype Pollution)
description: The software receives input from an upstream component that specifies attributes that are intended to be initialized or updated in an object prototype, but it does not properly control modifications to the prototype.
fix: Freeze Object.prototype. Use Object.create(null) for maps. Validate and sanitize property keys. Use lodash.set with care. Implement allowlist of allowed properties.

name: CWE-117: Improper Output Neutralization for Logs
description: The software does not neutralize or incorrectly neutralizes output that is written to logs, allowing an attacker to forge log entries or inject malicious content into logs.
fix: Encode newlines and special characters in log output. Use structured logging (JSON). Validate and sanitize all log input. Implement log integrity checks.

name: CWE-918 Cloud Metadata SSRF
description: SSRF targeting cloud provider metadata services to steal credentials and instance information.
fix: Block access to 169.254.169.254 (AWS), metadata.google.internal (GCP), 169.254.169.254 (Azure). Implement egress filtering. Use IMDSv2 on AWS.

name: CWE-77: Command Injection (Argument Injection)
description: The software constructs a command string for execution by a separate component in another control sphere, but it does not properly delimit the intended arguments.
fix: Use execFile/execSync with array arguments. Avoid shell=True. Validate and sanitize all arguments. Use allowlist of allowed commands.

name: CWE-400: Uncontrolled Resource Consumption (DoS)
description: The software does not properly control the allocation and maintenance of a limited resource, thereby enabling an actor to influence the amount of resources consumed, eventually leading to the exhaustion of available resources.
fix: Implement rate limiting. Set timeouts on all operations. Limit request sizes. Use connection pooling. Implement circuit breakers. Monitor resource usage.

name: CWE-269: Improper Privilege Management
description: The software does not properly assign, modify, track, or check privileges for an actor, creating an unintended sphere of control for that actor.
fix: Implement principle of least privilege. Regular privilege audits. Use role-based access. Remove unnecessary admin rights. Implement just-in-time access.

name: CWE-327: Use of a Broken or Risky Cryptographic Algorithm
description: The use of a broken or risky cryptographic algorithm is an unnecessary risk that may result in the exposure of sensitive information.
fix: Use AES-256-GCM for encryption. Use bcrypt/argon2 for passwords. Use TLS 1.3. Avoid MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4. Implement proper key management.

name: CWE-319: Cleartext Transmission of Sensitive Information
description: The software transmits sensitive or security-critical data in cleartext in a communication channel that can be sniffed by unauthorized actors.
fix: Enforce HTTPS everywhere. Use HSTS. Encrypt sensitive data at rest. Use secure protocols (SSH, SFTP). Implement certificate pinning.

name: CWE-798: Use of Hard-coded Credentials
description: The software contains hard-coded credentials, such as a password or cryptographic key, which it uses for its own inbound authentication, outbound communication to external components, or encryption of internal data.
fix: Never hardcode credentials. Use environment variables. Use secret managers (HashiCorp Vault, AWS Secrets Manager). Rotate keys regularly.

name: CWE-312: Cleartext Storage of Sensitive Information
description: The application stores sensitive information in cleartext within a resource that might be accessible to another control sphere.
fix: Encrypt sensitive data at rest. Use key management systems. Don't store secrets in config files. Use database encryption. Implement field-level encryption.

name: CWE-444: Inconsistent Interpretation of HTTP Requests (HTTP Request Smuggling)
description: The system acts as an HTTP intermediary (proxy/firewall/WAF) and inconsistently interprets HTTP requests, allowing an attacker to smuggle requests past security controls.
fix: Use consistent parsing. Disable pipeline. Implement strict header validation. Use modern load balancers. Normalize requests before routing.
